text: An incision is made in front of the lateral tendon of the pectoralis muscle on the left side.
The subcutaneous tissue is dissected in front of the fascia of the muscle, reaching the clavicle bone and then entering into the neck space.
Here the proper area is found in between the two heads of the sternocleidomastoid muscle.
The anterior sternocleidomastoid muscle head is retracted upward with the strap muscles and parathyroidal muscles.
The prethyroid muscles are retracted anteriorly from the thyroid and a self retaining retractor is placed.
The robot is docked from the patient's right side and the ports are placed inside the incision.
The assistant retracts the tissue downward as the section begins around the thyroid.
The vessels of the lower pole of the thyroid are divided with the harmonic device.
The enlarged gland is progressively removed using a combination of blunt dissection and the harmonic device.
The gland is removed from the superior portion of the thymus
